message:
Connectors: fix message index assignment consistency (claude_code, codex, gemini)

Apply the same index re-assignment pattern established in amp.rs, cline.rs,
and opencode.rs to the remaining three connectors for consistency.

Problem:
When since_ts filtering is applied, messages are skipped during iteration.
Using the enumeration index directly (idx) as the message index would create
gaps in the sequence (e.g., [0, 2, 5, 8] instead of [0, 1, 2, 3]).

Solution:
1. Use _idx (underscore prefix) to indicate the enumeration index is unused
2. Set msg.idx = 0 as a placeholder during initial message creation
3. After all filtering is complete, re-assign sequential indices with a
   final pass: `for (i, msg) in messages.iter_mut().enumerate()`

This ensures:
- Message indices are always sequential [0, 1, 2, ...]
- No gaps from filtered messages
- Consistent behavior across all 6 connectors
- Clippy is happy (no unused variable warnings)

Files modified:
- src/connectors/claude_code.rs: Both JSONL and JSON branches
- src/connectors/codex.rs: Both JSONL and legacy JSON branches
- src/connectors/gemini.rs: Main message processing loop

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
